mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points quarterly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, generating positive equity momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series deviates from its 12-month moving average by more than 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization improvements, triggering short-term positive equity repricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 following positive industrial production revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profit margins, resulting in negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 70% of quarters where GDP growth is below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports higher wage-driven consumption and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when payroll growth is driven solely by temporary government hiring.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained levels of industrial production above the 2007 peak indicate structural supply capacity recovery, providing a stable foundation for positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months where industrial production exceeds the 2007 peak level.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals immediate consumer pullback, triggering short-term negative equity repricing during periods of high market volatility.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances following retail sales declines when market volatility indices exceed 25.
